body:
Boston Globe "only the GOP can prevent a government shutdown."
---
[](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d7bc/2b07114c/b9e014a1/731baa18/3812972962/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/no-gop-shutdown?source=PT-20180118-UrgentAlert_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")
**_Boston Globe: "Only the GOP can prevent a government shutdown"_
[Sign now: tell Republicans not to shut down the government >>](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d7bc/2b07114c/b9e014a1/731baa18/3812972962/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/no-gop-shutdown?source=PT-20180118-UrgentAlert_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")**
Team,

The federal government will shut down at midnight on Friday without a new budget -- and the _Boston Globe_ is reporting that "only the GOP can prevent a government shutdown."

**Republicans control the White House, the Senate and the House. They have the power to decide whether to shut down the government -- and we have only 1 day to stop them.**

We've seen this playbook before. We know Republicans are willing to let government funding expire -- even if it means leaving thousands of federal employees unpaid and out of work and abandoning millions of Americans without critical government services.

[**Add your name right now: tell Republicans not to shut down the federal government >>**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d7bc/2b07114c/b9e014a1/731baa18/3812972962/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/no-gop-shutdown?source=PT-20180118-UrgentAlert_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

The GOP's refusal to compromise and avert a shutdown isn't governance -- it's reckless obstruction with no consideration for the consequences.

When the GOP shut down the federal government in 2013, it cost taxpayers billions of dollars, along with worry and inconvenience for so many Americans. It's unbelievable that Republicans are considering putting the country through that again.

**We must speak out before federal funding expires at midnight on Friday. Add your name: tell Republicans to avoid a shutdown now:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/no-gop-shutdown**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d7bc/2b07114c/b9e014a1/731baa18/3812972962/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/no-gop-shutdown?source=PT-20180118-UrgentAlert_DS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

Thank you,

Team DSCC

**A quick note on donations, team:**

Republicans are once again threatening to shut down the government and Senate Democrats need your support to fight back. We have to show the GOP the consequences they'll face in November for a reckless shutdown. Your gift to Senate Democrats will help prove we're ready to hold Republicans accountable.

[**Contribute**](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-democrats?refcode=DPE-20180118-UrgentAlert_DS-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")

120 Maryland Ave. NE
Washington, DC 20002** Paid for by the DSCC, dscc.org,
and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
The DSCC is the only committee solely dedicated to supporting Democrats in the U.S. Senate. This message was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com because you are a part of the DSCC's grassroots network. Please add us to your address book or approved senders list to make sure you keep receiving DSCC updates. Emails are a critical way for us to stay in touch with our grassroots supporters. 99% of the DSCC's contributions come from grassroots donors giving less than $200. We also strive to keep you updated on issues related to the Democratic Party's values.

If you'd like to take a break from DSCC emails, [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d7bc/2b07114c/b9e014a1/731baa1a/3812972962/VEsA/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/take-an-email-break?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com").

If you'd like to make a contribution to support us, please [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d7bc/2b07114c/b9e014a1/731baa15/3812972962/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/supportdscc?refcode=DPE-disclaimer"). Together, we're going to take back the Senate, but we'll need the help of every single grassroots supporter to do it. If you'd like to stop receiving all emails from the DSCC, please [click here](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d7bc/2b07114c/b9e014a1/731baa14/3812972962/VEsO/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/content/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"). name: Jeanne Shaheen
subject: Need 32 signers from 3264
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shaheen for Senate
body:
Friends -
Almost one year ago, we witnessed Donald Trump, a man who has insulted women, threatened to limit their access to safe and affordable health care, and even bragged about sexual assault, get sworn in as president of the United States.
It's a scary reality, but here's what gives me hope: The day after the inauguration, _millions_ of women from all over the country - and the _world_ - came together to march in Washington and show that we were not going to back down in the face of Trump's anti-women agenda. And one year later, women are coming together again to say we're still putting up a fight.
Speaking out has never been so important - so before the march this weekend, I'd like to hear where you stand:
**[Click here to take a survey on Trump and his extreme anti-women agenda.
](http://surveys.signforgood.com/CJSWomensMarch2018?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk823-su-hassan-nd)**
Last year, we swore in New Hampshire's first all-female, all-Democrat congressional delegation. We have a lot of issues to address, and we will be fierce advocates day in and day out for the best interests of New Hampshire.
If 2017 showed us anything, it's that it takes women all over the country coming together to try to hold Trump and his administration accountable. That's why I need you to show me where you stand on Trump's anti-women agenda before the march:
**[Take the survey on Trump's anti-women agenda!
](http://surveys.signforgood.com/CJSWomensMarch2018?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&code=jtk823-su-hassan-nd)**
Thank you,
Jeanne

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.

name: The White House
subject: The 45th March for Life
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b1ecf39aa6d001744c2369e4b6c6a778696fd17a917717631919d58baf741465bb518659397dd801695cd89a26c19fbbf2e62ec3864b6eae

The 45th March for Life

Thirty-eight years ago, Nellie Gray left her job as a Labor Department attorney to start a small nonprofit organization. Today, that group—the March for Life Education and Defense Fund—is responsible for organizing the largest annual pro-life rally in the world.

President Donald J. Trump and Vice President Mike Pence will join the event via video satellite from the White House Rose Garden this afternoon.

Last year, an estimated 500,000 people attended the march to support a cause that has grown immensely since the first event in 1974. The annual trek along the National Mall to the U.S. Capitol is the most visible symbol of March for Life's vision: "a world where every human life is valued and protected."

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b1ecf39aa6d00174bb36db3744eea5f9f4611591c6217244bcfb8aeb5d38ef2cdb6a8451da5e0556975f5f2eebae80b9d1e24fb59e6715a9
Watch live at 12:15 p.m. ET.

A month after the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act

President Trump signed the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act into law on December 22. Yesterday, he traveled to Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, to talk about the impact the legislation has had in just a few short weeks.

The stories keep rolling in. More than 2 million American workers have gotten a “Trump bonus” or “Trump pay raise.” The net tax burden on American families has fallen by $3.2 trillion. Chrysler has announced that it will move truck production from Mexico back to the United States.

H&K Equipment, where the President spoke yesterday, is investing back into its own company at a rate 50 percent higher than last year—and directly credits tax cuts for providing them with the ability to expense 100 percent of the investments they make in new equipment.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b1ecf39aa6d00174160c3af014581ccf73d43515de74c546c0e34ff6b8d0495c8398f836c919377657e072b701aea75db6fb0c656b2eeee3
Watch President Trump tour the H&K Equipment Company in Pittsburgh.

A year to remember

Tomorrow marks one full year since Donald J. Trump swore the oath of office on the steps of the U.S. Capitol to become the 45th President of the United States.

“Today’s ceremony . . . has very special meaning,” President Trump told the country. “Because today we are not merely transferring power from one Administration to another, or from one party to another – but we are transferring power from Washington, D.C. and giving it back to you, the American People.”

In the year since, President Trump has met with thousands of Americans from coast to coast, hosted numerous foreign dignitaries in Washington, and traveled to 13 foreign countries to represent the United States abroad.

Watch:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b1ecf39aa6d00174e7fe8495499d625d1b333dfa7b17e91861b932648f97744317312e5fe7e253ef7f7b68308dd789f0034ecad840357197
President Trump’s first year—in 3 1⁄2 minutes .

Bonus:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b1ecf39aa6d00174ae5184c48a0875ec5985c5d38f1f2480f20784bb25957a21049dd56056e3e97b539c67b563c830a43f3fb674df9617c9
See the full list of the Trump Administration’s year one accomplishments.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump at the Pentagon | January 18, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

Today, President Trump will address March for Life Participants and pro-life leaders in the Rose Garden.

Later, the President will meet with his national security team.

In the evening, the Vice President and Second Lady will depart Washington, D.C., on Air Force Two en route to Clare, Ireland for a refueling stop before arriving in Cairo, Egypt.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b1ecf39aa6d001745e56b410190b15a3a6f6984aa67689023e5f4a24f7108717e6d86256573604143b01fdc04f2c53cbd75eb9e549e2b19b
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=b1ecf39aa6d00174f63e850e2343048f7823db611e36b9a7502ce58f07d77df10c08c272419d027a3915fea6627798b4ed567025cc83fa1b
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d4a02e1195e29f3eedf1308622eaebc9388675b6edf032c3347a4207d856c9e68338a989b295a2b5697e722b18a4429070
Unsubscribe

name: Team Kaine
subject: Stand up to the Trump administration with us
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
Derek -- It's been nearly a week since we last emailed you about our offshore drilling concerns, and we have nothing to report.

**That's right, we've gotten complete radio silence from the Trump administration. We can't say we're surprised. Avoiding responsible governing in favor of showmanship and Twitter? Not exactly a new and exciting tactic for these guys.
**
But it's irresponsible nonetheless. Because Virginia's leaders -- and even more importantly, Virginia's citizens -- have spoken up on this issue. On Saturday, we sent you a key survey to get your take and thousands of respondents weighed in. It just couldn't be any clearer: Expanding offshore drilling will hurt Virginia, and everyone knows it. The move by the Trump administration to exempt Florida from this major change in policy while ignoring other states is hypocritical at best and brazenly partisan at worst. But of course, partisanship *isn't* actually the worst thing here -- negative impact on our coastal communities and naval operations is.

**We shouldn't stand for this.[Add your name to tell Secretary of the Interior Ryan Zinke and President Trump that Virginia deserves the same consideration as any other coastal state.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=JYqGlZjSb5jXoYItPHtVFdsVt7hiyvFh)
**
If we've learned anything in the last year, it's that collective action gets things done. Speaking up, signing petitions, making calls -- all of it makes a difference.

**So please, lend your voice to this critical fight:

[ Sign this petition to protect Virginia's coasts from offshore drilling.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=u2q1PI2Nt9fz5Ko723xDK9sVt7hiyvFh)**

Thank you for all you do. We'll be in touch with updates.

-Team Kaine _\---------- Forwarded message ----------_
From: **Team Kaine** (info@timkaine.com)
Subject: SURVEY: We want to hear from you about offshore drilling >>
|
---
A lot's been happening with offshore drilling in the last week. Here's the short of it:

Last week, Trump announced that he would be expanding offshore drilling anywhere and everywhere -- a move that would negatively affect countless communities and threaten Virginia's coastal tourism and naval operations in Hampton Roads. (It's also completely unnecessary, but that's a whole other email.)

Then, after the Governor of Florida spoke up, Secretary of the Interior Ryan Zinke announced that there would be no drilling off the coast of Florida. As Zinke said, "local voice matters" -- and he had heard loud and clear that offshore drilling would be bad for Florida.

**Sure, that seems right. But what about other coastal states -- like, say, Virginia? Both Terry McAuliffe and Ralph Northam have spoken up, too, and we have yet to hear of any exemptions for the Commonwealth. So it begs the question: Why exactly is Florida the exception here?
**
Could it possibly be that the Mar-a-Lago resort would be negatively impacted? (Or the "Winter White House" as some call it...) Could it be because the Governor of Florida is a Republican, while Virginia's leaders are Democrats? Could it be that Florida is a key state that the Republican administration is eager to have on its side? **No matter which way you cut it, as a recent Washington Post editorial put it, excluding Florida "sure looks partisan."**

**So here's what we need from you: If Secretary Zinke is determined to listen to local voices, we must speak up.****[Will you fill out this quick, one-minute survey to share your opinion on the recent offshore drilling news?](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=4NYhtf28fCqoXI7DMpDt4NsVt7hiyvFh)**

Thanks -- we really appreciate your input.

-Team Kaine

body:
This is a critical moment to show you're standing with Democrats.
---
**Republicans are hours away from forcing a government shutdown.

Chip in $3 right now to replace them with Democrats.
**[DONATE](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/40522381/4067b0a/4b768a899/7e5112a5/1087612281/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/govt-shutdown?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=em_20180119_dnc2_nd&source=em_20180119_dnc2_nd&refcode=em_20180119_dnc2_nd")**
**
Derek --

It looks increasingly possible that in less than 10 hours, Republicans will shut down the federal government.

Bizarrely enough, Republicans are trying to make the case that this outcome would be the Democrats' fault. That's what Donald Trump has been tweeting, and no doubt that's what Fox News will be saying in the coming days.

That is, of course, an absurd argument. Republicans control the:
* White House
* House of Representatives
* Senate

If the GOP wanted the government to stay open, it'd stay open. But that's not going to stop them from making the ridiculous argument that this is all somehow the Democrats' fault -- and as we know all too well, unless all of us stand together and stand up for the lawmakers who are holding the line for our values, the GOP might win this fight.

**Right now is a critical moment to show that you're standing with Democrats -- chip in $3 and stand up to the GOP.**

Hello --

Thanks to Donald Trump, Florida's richest corporations are going to take home even more sacks of money than they usually do.

But we're going to stand up to Trump with the boldest progressive tax proposal in the country, so we can invest $1 billion in our working families.

My "Fair Share for Florida's Future" plan will build world-class schools, give public school teachers a raise, strengthen and expand early childhood education, and make SHOP 2.0 vocational classes more widely available.

**[We can make the largest corporations in our state return some of the windfalls they got in Trump's tax plan and reinvest it in Florida's working families, but only if we stand together -- so will you add your name and become a citizen sponsor?](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31ed/9e00690/431f5a89/2403ece0/3208251422/VEsE/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/become-a-citizen-sponnsor?utm_campaign=em18_180119_TaxPla_acq_gq_act__AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&source=em18_180119_TaxPla_acq_gq_act__AG&utm_content=2+-+We+can+make+the+largest+corporations+in+")**

name: Greg Pence
subject: Today’s March for Life - Our Shared Hoosier Values
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greg Pence for Congress |
body:
I’m an unwavering pro-life American. I always have been - and, I always will be. | | |
---|---|---
|
---

Friend,

**I’m an unwavering pro-life American.** I always have been - and, I always will be.

As a father of four and a grandfather of five, I know I’m blessed. Every life is a gift from God.

Today, pro-life advocates from across our country, including hundreds from Indiana, will gather on the National Mall in Washington, D.C. for the 45th Annual March for Life.

I’m proud of these people for standing up for their beliefs - for believing that every human life, including the unborn, should be protected.

Safe travels for all Hoosiers heading back from D.C. to Indiana after the March for Life, including the St. Bartholomew Youth Ministry Group who I visited with ahead of their departure on Wednesday evening.

Thank you for supporting our shared, conservative values.

God Bless,
Greg Pence

body:
**Forrester Statement on Governor Sununu's School Infrastructure Safety Fund**
**For Immediate Release:** 1/19/2018
**Contact:** Patrick Hynes, (202) 321-5310
CONCORD - NH GOP Chair Jeanie Forrester released the following statement regarding Governor Sununu's School Infrastructure Safety Fund:
“Governor Chris Sununu's leadership on establishing the School Infrastructure Safety Fund continues to expand upon the impressive leadership he and Commissioner Frank Edelblut have shown on Education. Today, Governor Sununu spoke with the Joint Fiscal Committee on investing over $10 million into 170 school infrastructure projects throughout the State of New Hampshire. These projects will target necessary updates, combating asbestos and lead paint exposure in schools, making New Hampshire's schools the safest in the nation. This investment of state funds will go to great lengths to lessen the burden on local property tax payers, giving the people of New Hampshire even more of their hard-earned tax dollars."

Peter,

2018 could be a record-breaking year for pro-choice Democratic women.

The highest number of women ever are running for House seats. We're looking at endorsing historic numbers of candidates for state legislatures, governors' seats, and even Congress.

But these numbers only become wins if we step up now.

Last January, our Membership Week broke records. But I think we can raise the bar even higher this year.

**[Donate $3 before midnight to become a member for 2018 and help make this Membership Week the biggest one yet.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6b44/24c69fd9/133726954/1895dd26/785250112/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180112_email_JanMembershipWeek?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+if+consinConsGroup9050+before+mid&utm_campaign=WQEFRM33N_digital_email_20180119_LastCallJanuaryMembership_inact&source=WQEFRM33N_digital_email_20180119_LastCallJanuaryMembership_inact")**

Looking at the numbers from last year, it's obvious how energized this community was to stand up to the new Trump Administration.

Now, we're finally in the year of the midterm elections. And because of all the work we did to recruit and train potential candidates last year, we are in a strong position to put women in power who can stop Republicans all the way from the White House down to the states (where they continue to push radical anti-choice policies).

We're going to support a lot of women this year. Potentially a record-breaking number.

But whether or not we can do it really depends on if you're still in this fight like you were a year ago.

**Donate $3 before midnight to become a member for 2018 and help break records with our candidates:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/January-Membership-Week](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6b44/24c69fd9/133726954/1895dd27/785250112/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180112_email_JanMembershipWeek?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgJanuaryMembershi&utm_campaign=WQEFRM33N_digital_email_20180119_LastCallJanuaryMembership_inact&source=WQEFRM33N_digital_email_20180119_LastCallJanuaryMembership_inact")**

Thanks -- and go team!

Emily Cain
Executive Director, EMILY's List

P.S. name: DNC Events
subject: [WOMEN'S MARCH] Join us tomorrow >>
body:
Your state party is looking for volunteers who can help at the Women's March.
---
Hey, Derek!

Tomorrow marks one year since women and allies came together in Washington, D.C. and communities across the country for the historic Women's March.

With united voices, inspiring speeches, and pink knitted hats, we made a statement of resistance against the discriminatory and offensive behavior of Donald Trump and those who enable him.

**As we plan to gather together again this weekend, your state party is looking for volunteers who can help at the Women's March in Virginia to make sure people are committed to voting in such an important election year.**

Will you come out and volunteer, Derek?

**Women's March Event**
**When:** this weekend
**Where:** your community

**[RSVP](https://www.womensmarch.com/)**
This November, we have the chance to unseat Republicans and elect Democrats who will resist Trump and the GOP -- but it won't happen without your help.

**[RSVP today to volunteer during the Women's March this weekend.](https://www.womensmarch.com/)**

name: Martin Heinrich
subject: Trump Wants a Fight
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARTIN HEINRICH FOR SENATE
body:
|
---

| | | Friend,

At midnight tonight, the government is slated to shut down. The vast majority of members on both sides of the aisle support a bipartisan agreement that has been on the table for a week. This agreement would fund the government and provide support for key bipartisan programs like CHIP and DACA, but hardline conservatives and an erratic White House have rejected it and forced Republicans away from the negotiating table. And because the Trump Administration is so averse to cleaning up their own mess or even governing, it may result in a government shutdown tonight.

The real truth is that their pollsters are telling them that if they try to make this showdown about demagoguing 'illegal immigration,' then they might gain the upper hand. They want you to forget they spent the entire first year of the Trump Administration trying to take away Americans' health care and ramming through tax breaks for special interests on a party-line vote. Now veterans benefits, our national security and other critical real life issues hang in the balance.

Both Democrats and Republicans want to undo the sequester, which is hurting our military and other federal investments critical to our communities and economic growth. Both parties know funding is necessary to fight wildfires, to combat the opioid epidemic, to support our veterans, and to replenish pension funds. And there is bipartisan support growing daily to reauthorize the Children's Health Insurance Program and to protect Dreamers from the Trump Administration. But Republican leaders refuse to bring these bipartisan priorities - left to languish over the last year - to the floor for a vote.

We are now in this position because Republicans haven't addressed these bipartisan issues and have been unwilling to pass funding bills for longer than a few weeks at a time. Just yesterday the Pentagon warned us about how this short-term, fits-and-starts approach to funding the government is damaging our national security.

There is a logjam of bare minimum governance issues that this Administration and their allies in Congress refuse to address because their right-wing base wants chaos and this White House is happy to give it to them. Last week, President Trump said he would accept whatever the parties agreed to. The next day he lost his temper in a vulgar meltdown when the bipartisan agreement was presented to him. It makes no sense and he can't seem to take yes for an answer.

I strongly believe that instead of posturing about which party comes out on top over a showdown like this, we should embrace the bipartisanship that is literally sitting right in front of us. Doesn't everybody win if we solve real problems and govern? The problem is that it appears the Trump White House would rather fight than win.

Sincerely,
Martin
---

---

| PAID FOR BY MARTIN HEINRICH FOR SENATE

name: Dr. James Zogby, The Sanders Institute
subject: The Immigration Debate We Must Not Lose
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
#
Dear Friend,
The debate over US immigration policy is a very personal one for me. It's about my family's history and the hardships they faced coming to America. It is also about who we are and who we aspire to be as an American people.
[**_I would like to share my immigration story with you; you can read it on The Sanders Institute website._**](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ad57/bed43eb/33686951/75feb662/3321306474/VEsH/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/the-immigration-debate-we-must-not-lose-")
The language we are hearing today against different groups of people is nothing new. In the 1920's, the US Congress was in the grips of a nativist xenophobic fervor. Congressional debates termed Syrians as "parasites" with one Senator saying "we don't need any more Syrian trash coming here." Sound familiar?
I am the son of an undocumented immigrant from a once-reviled country and a member of a family that benefited from provisions that allowed for families to be unified. All of them are proud contributing members of American life.
What, to me, is remarkable about our story is that it is not remarkable, at all. Millions of Americans can tell the same story because it is the American story. It is who we are. [**_We want to hear your story of immigration to the United States, whether your family has been here for generations, you're a new American or a Dreamer. Join us on Facebook and post your written statements or videos to our page._**](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ad57/bed43eb/33686951/75feb66d/3321306474/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/sandersinstitute/")
As I said, this issue is not new. As I look at those who are leading the charge against immigration and those working to reframe the debate casting immigrants and refugees in disgraceful and racist terms, I see descendants of Germans, Irish, Italians, and Jews--all of whom were once reviled, locked out, and victims of bigotry.
In every generation, there are two threads of our national narrative--the one that advocates for openness and the other that is exclusionary. In the past, the one that advocated for openness has won out. It is our fight today to make sure that it wins again. The soul of America is at stake. We dare not lose.
In Solidarity,
James Zogby, Chairman of the Board, The Sanders Institute

BURLINGTON, VT 05401

We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6b3e/24c69fd9/133eccb10/1895cd7c/2304987880/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQENEM35N_digital_email_20180119_RisingStar1Kicker_inact&source=WQENEM35N_digital_email_20180119_RisingStar1Kicker_inact")

Peter,

It's the last day to submit nominations for the Gabrielle Giffords Rising Star Award!

Our past Rising Star recipients have done amazing things in their communities. Our first Rising Star, Stacey Abrams, is now running for governor of Georgia!

As a former state legislator, I can tell you firsthand that a national award like this means a whole lot to our women working in state and local offices. It's an awesome way to thank the women in our communities fighting for us every day and show that we're with them.

I can't wait to see who this year's nominees will be.

**Do you know a pro-choice Democratic woman in state or local office doing incredible work? Nominate her and help decide who the next Gabrielle Giffords Rising Star will be:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Nominate-the-2018-Rising-Star](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6b3e/24c69fd9/133eccb10/1895cd7d/2304987880/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20180112_email_RisingStar1?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgNominatethe2018R&utm_campaign=WQENEM35N_digital_email_20180119_RisingStar1Kicker_inact&source=WQENEM35N_digital_email_20180119_RisingStar1Kicker_inact")**

Thanks -- and go team!

Emily Cain
Executive Director, EMILY's List

_\-----------Original Message----------
From: Geri Prado, EMILY's List
Sent: Fri, Jan 12, 2018 at 5:55 p.m._
Peter,

Nominations for the annual Gabrielle Giffords Rising Star Award are now open!

This award is something we give out every year to a woman currently serving in state or local office who has a bright career in public service ahead of her.

Last year was huge for women in state and local politics, with so many of them setting up critical protections for their communities against the Trump agenda and so many new faces running and winning for the first time.

If you know an elected woman who is a leader in your community, we'd love to hear about her.

**[Who do you think should be the next Gabrielle Giffords Rising Star Award recipient? Nominate a pro-choice Democratic woman in state or local office who is doing great work.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6b3e/24c69fd9/133eccb10/1895cd7e/2304987880/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20180112_email_RisingStar1?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+Who+do+you+think+should+be+the+next+Gabr&utm_campaign=WQENEM35N_digital_email_20180119_RisingStar1Kicker_inact&source=WQENEM35N_digital_email_20180119_RisingStar1Kicker_inact")**

The Rising Star Award recognizes pro-choice Democratic women serving in state legislatures, mayors' offices, city councils, school boards, and beyond. It's a way to say thank you for her work and show that we will support her wherever her career takes her next.

Past winners include Philadelphia City Councilwoman Helen Gym, Colorado Speaker of the House Crisanta Duran, Boston City Councilwoman Ayanna Pressley, and former Georgia House Minority Leader and current gubernatorial candidate Stacey Abrams.

They all have something in common: They tirelessly fight for women, families, and their communities. And we know the 2018 Rising Star is out there doing the same thing right now.

But we need your help to find her and celebrate her work.

**Nominate a pro-choice Democratic woman serving in state or local office for the 2018 Gabrielle Giffords Rising Star Award:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Nominate-the-2018-Rising-Star](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6b3e/24c69fd9/133eccb10/1895cd7f/2304987880/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/s/20180112_email_RisingStar1?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgNominatethe2018R&utm_campaign=WQENEM35N_digital_email_20180119_RisingStar1Kicker_inact&source=WQENEM35N_digital_email_20180119_RisingStar1Kicker_inact")**

Thanks,

Geri Prado

**NH REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, JANUARY 19TH**
|
---
**_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**

**Today Governor Sununu spoke to the Joint Fiscal Committee about investing over $10 million into 170 school infrastructure projects throughout the state. These projects will target necessary updates, combat asbestos and lead paint exposure in schools and really work towards making New Hampshire schools the safest in the nation.**

**What a breath of fresh air--to have a Governor who doesn't just "talk the talk" but really "walks the walk!" In the six years I served in the State Senate, I can tell you there was always a lot of talk from the corner office about getting things done, but really not a lot getting accomplished.**

**From sending $38 million back to our cities and towns for road and bridge repair, to doubling our investment in tackling the opioid crisis and creating a scholarship fund to increase accessibility for NH students, to making our schools safer, what difference one year and one great Republican Governor can make!**

clean_body: | | nh.gop NH REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, JANUARY 19TH | A Note From Jeanie Dear Derek, Today Governor Sununu spoke to the Joint Fiscal Committee about investing over $10 million into 170 school infrastructure projects throughout the state. These projects will target necessary updates, combat asbestos and lead paint exposure in schools and really work towards making New Hampshire schools the safest in the nation. What a breath of fresh air--to have a Governor who doesn't just "talk the talk" but really "walks the walk!" In the six years I served in the State Senate, I can tell you there was always a lot of talk from the corner office about getting things done, but really not a lot getting accomplished. From sending $38 million back to our cities and towns for road and bridge repair, to doubling our investment in tackling the opioid crisis and creating a scholarship fund to increase accessibility for NH students, to making our schools safer, what difference one year and one great Republican Governor can make! To see everything that's been accomplished in Governor Sununu's first year, check out. Sincerely, Jeanie Join the Grassroots Circle!
